production_mode: generated_actor_mediated_world (default of resolve_consequence_mode, semantic_consequences.py:81-84; the fixed ontology in semantic_consequences.py is the BASELINE, doc semantic_consequences.py:31-35)
canonical_execution_chain: 1. rollout.RolloutEngine.run_branch (rollout.py:29) — THE canonical event loop: pop event, advance clock, run applicable operators, append each StateDelta to branch.log, queue delta.follow_up_events (rollout.py:34-73)
2. events.EventQueue.next_event (events.py:109) — pops next timestamped Event (heap keyed on ts, events.py:49-52), re-arms hazards that fired (events.py:115-121)
3. operator dispatch loop (rollout.py:52-53) — for each op: op.applicable(world, ev) then op.run(world, ev, rng); run() is propose->validate->apply (transitions.py:107-119)

4. generated_world.GeneratedActorInvocationOperator.run (generated_world.py:710) on a ctrl_invoke_actor event — the actor's perceived world changed; rebuild view + invoke their persistent policy; deliberate inaction is first-class (generated_world.py:743-747)

5. qualitative_actor.QualitativeActorPolicyRuntime.decide (qualitative_actor.py:867) — routes qualitative-vs-numeric (qualitative_actor.py:880-896), builds ActorView, runs the LLM, resolves chosen text to a TypedAction via _resolve (qualitative_actor.py:968); returns (TypedAction, ActionPosterior, DecisionTrace). Base numeric path: phase4_execution.ActorPolicyRuntime.decide (phase4_execution.py:68)

6. phase4_execution.ActorPolicyRuntime.execute (phase4_execution.py:97) — rechecks ACTUAL feasibility (attempted-but-blocked is a real outcome, phase4_execution.py:102-120); writes ONLY the actor's own current_action/past_actions/resources/commitments (phase4_execution.py:130-137); then consequences
7. phase4_execution.ActorPolicyRuntime._apply_consequences (phase4_execution.py:174; generated branch 192-216) — the action->world seam; dispatches on consequence_mode

8. generated_world.GeneratedActionCompiler.compile (generated_world.py:414) — chosen action -> validated kernel ops against the BRANCH scenario schema; untrusted LLM proposal with one vocab-repair round (generated_world.py:447-477), deterministic scaffolding fallback (generated_world.py:483-496)

9. generated_world.execute_kernel_ops (generated_world.py:323) — applies each op via the CLOSED KERNEL registry (generated_world.py:310-316); k_emit_semantic_event (generated_world.py:211) appends a SemanticWorldEvent to world.semantic_log and returns a ctrl_semantic_event Event carrying the event dict as payload (generated_world.py:240-252); every mutation lands a delta.change on the SAME StateDelta

10. StateDelta.follow_up_events assigned (phase4_execution.py:153, generated mode uses events=list(semantic_events) at phase4_execution.py:147) and QUEUED by RolloutEngine (rollout.py:60-73) — validated against the event registry, no time travel (rollout.py:62), horizon-capped by the queue

11. generated_world.GeneratedSemanticEventOperator.run (generated_world.py:554) on ctrl_semantic_event — route_semantic_event (generated_world.py:506) yields per-recipient ctrl_deliver_observation events; discover_causal_frontier (generated_world.py:604) yields ctrl_invoke_actor events for causally-engaged actors; both ride delta.follow_up_events (generated_world.py:572)

12. generated_world.GeneratedObservationDeliveryOperator.run (generated_world.py:662) on ctrl_deliver_observation — publishes an InformationItem and world.information.expose(recipient,...) into the recipient's information ledger (generated_world.py:675-683), then schedules the recipient's ctrl_invoke_actor via _invocation_event (generated_world.py:685)

13. generated_world.GeneratedActorInvocationOperator.run (generated_world.py:710) on the recipient's ctrl_invoke_actor — RECURSION back to step 5: the recipient runs THEIR own runtime.decide; if they act, execute() emits new semantic events and the cascade continues under budgets

TERMINAL: contracts.OutcomeContract.project reads the answer via make_generated_predicate_readout (generated_world.py:805) which runs evaluate_predicate (scenario_schema.py:245) over the FROZEN outcome_predicates against world.objects — a read-only readout, never a resolver draw (rollout.py:104-106)

scenario_world_generation.entry: materialize._bind_scenario_schema (materialize.py:391), called from run_from_plan (materialize.py:466) and MatchedEvaluator.from_plan; only active in generated_actor_mediated_world mode (materialize.py:398-399). Schema is supplied via plan.scenario_schema / plan.provenance['scenario_schema'] (materialize.py:404-406) or generated by scenario_schema.SchemaCompiler.compile (scenario_schema.py:399) from question+entities+institutions+evidence
schema_objects: scenario_schema.ScenarioSemanticModel dataclass (scenario_schema.py:53) — the branch-local, frozen-then-extensible world semantics
entity_types / fact_types / relation_types (scenario_schema.py:63-66) — scenario-named typed record shapes with field kinds in FIELD_KINDS str|float|int|bool|list|id|time (scenario_schema.py:41)

semantic_event_types (scenario_schema.py:67) — the scenario's OWN event vocabulary {type_id: {description, fields, typical_visibility}}; the ONE global fixture is UNMODELED_EVENT_TYPE scaffolding added at freeze (scenario_schema.py:46,151-155)
resource_definitions (scenario_schema.py:76) — {unit, conserved}; information_rules (scenario_schema.py:77) — channel/visibility/delay semantics read by the router (generated_world.py:521-529)
actor_roles (scenario_schema.py:78) — {role, why_consequential, affordance EXAMPLES (never a required menu, enforced scenario_schema.py:230-233)}
outcome_predicates (scenario_schema.py:81) — [{predicate_id, record_type, field, op:exists|eq|ne|in|gte|lte, value, option_true, option_false}] that resolve the question from the world
scenario_world_generation.validation: Deterministic gate scenario_schema.validate_scenario_schema (scenario_schema.py:184): rejects numeric-minting field/type names via _FORBIDDEN_FIELD (scenario_schema.py:32) and action->human-reaction coefficients via _REACTION_COEFF (scenario_schema.py:36), invalid field kinds (scenario_schema.py:179), non-executable institution aggregation kinds (scenario_schema.py:206-209), dangling decision_record_type/outcome record types (scenario_schema.py:213,224), and initial-world outcome smuggling via validate_initial_records (scenario_schema.py:237). auto_repair_schema (scenario_schema.py:313) makes honesty-preserving mechanical repairs (labels constraints unresolved, drops holderless institutions LOUDLY, auto-declares referenced record types) but NEVER repairs semantic violations. Non-binding LLM critic SchemaCompiler.criticize (scenario_schema.py:450) can veto only on verdict=='fatal' (scenario_schema.py:445). Then model.freeze() stamps a content_hash (scenario_schema.py:148-160)
scenario_world_generation.materialization: materialize.build_world (materialize.py:42) builds the base WorldState (entities/populations/relations/institutions/quantities/information ledger). _bind_scenario_schema attaches the frozen schema to base.scenario_schema (materialize.py:439) and REPLACES the contract readout with make_generated_predicate_readout + the predicates' own option labels (materialize.py:441-455). materialize.operators_from_plan (materialize.py:157) instantiates the three generated control-plane operators bound to the shared runtime + consequence_report (materialize.py:220-232). Worlds are deep-copied per particle so the schema is branch-local and extensible (materialize.py:396, generated_world.py doc:92)

consequence_execution.contract: GENERATED mode: generated_world.execute_kernel_ops(world, ops, ctx, delta) -> list[control-plane Event] (generated_world.py:323). Each op dispatches through the CLOSED KERNEL dict (generated_world.py:310-316: create_or_update_record, remove_record, create_or_remove_relation, emit_semantic_event, schedule_semantic_event, transfer_conserved_quantity, declare_schema_definition); every world mutation lands a delta.change on the ONE passed StateDelta; op failures quarantine LOUDLY onto ctx['quarantined'] and increment report['unsupported_semantics'] (generated_world.py:345-347), never crashing the branch; the return value is the control-plane events the caller queues. Direct effects (what the actor's own success makes true) apply deterministically; downstream effects come ONLY from other actors/institutions/mechanisms (doc semantic_consequences.py:7-9, prompt generated_world.py:355-359). FIXED baseline analog: semantic_consequences.execute_program(world, program, delta, report) -> events over the PRIMITIVES registry (semantic_consequences.py:631,602-619)
consequence_execution.how_actors_react: NOT a per-type handler — the actor loop IS the mechanism (generated_world.py doc:17-22). An emitted SemanticWorldEvent is routed to recipients by route_semantic_event using schema.information_rules (generated_world.py:506-536); the observation is delivered into the recipient's information ledger by GeneratedObservationDeliveryOperator (generated_world.py:662-683); the recipient's ctrl_invoke_actor fires and GeneratedActorInvocationOperator invokes THEIR runtime.decide (generated_world.py:738), where they interpret it and choose any action — including waiting (generated_world.py:743-747), including a novel action no menu anticipated (schema affordances ride only as examples, generated_world.py:732-736). No code path writes another consequential human's belief/support/choice
invariants_enforced: NO direct mind/reaction writes: the _MIND_WRITE regex (generated_world.py:318-320) blocks any op serializing to belief/support/compli/vote/approval/stance/trust/decision_of another actor; caught in execute_kernel_ops (generated_world.py:338-342), counted as human_reactions_attempted_directly; the pure-run invariant is report['human_reactions_written_directly']==0 (generated_world.py:22,72)

NO numeric minting: _FORBIDDEN_KEYS (semantic_consequences.py:67-69, imported into generated_world.py:37) rejects probab|pathway_progress|mode_progress|utility|belief_delta|terminal|forecast|outcome_label|ground_truth in any kernel field name (generated_world.py:121-122)

NO terminal/outcome writes: terminal|forecast|outcome_label|ground_truth are forbidden field names (semantic_consequences.py:68-69); the answer is only ever a READ-ONLY predicate readout over typed state (generated_world.py:805-816), never written into the world

SCHEMA FIDELITY: only declared record types (generated_world.py:137), declared semantic event types (generated_world.py:217), and declared fields (generated_world.py:123-126) may be written; anything new requires an explicit versioned, ancestry-preserving declare_schema_definition extension (generated_world.py:292, scenario_schema.py:471)
AUTHORITY: create/update/remove of an existing record requires created_by=='' or actor, or owner==actor (generated_world.py:146-148, 177-179)
CONSERVATION: transfer_conserved_quantity only from the actor's OWN holdings and only if sufficient (generated_world.py:271-278)

BUDGETS (truncate LOUDLY, never silently): max_semantic_events (generated_world.py:224-225), max_invocations_per_actor (generated_world.py:718-722), max_cascade_depth (generated_world.py:590), plus per-(actor,event) reconsideration dedup (generated_world.py:583-593)
consequence_execution.unmodeled_action_behavior: When GeneratedActionCompiler cannot compile an action's semantics (LLM absent/over-budget/unparseable, or deterministic path), it emits ONE emit_semantic_event of type UNMODELED_EVENT_TYPE='unmodeled_actor_action' (scenario_schema.py:46) preserving the EXACT action content in exact_content + structured_fields{action_name,content,target} (generated_world.py:483-491). That scaffolding type is schema-scoped, added at freeze() (scenario_schema.py:151-155). The event still enters the world and is routed/observed like any other (so other actors still see that the actor acted), but it is COUNTED as a fallback (report fallback_reasons + scaffolding flag, generated_world.py:244-246,492-495), never as modeled semantics. In fixed mode the analog marks program.unmodeled and records a record_observation (semantic_consequences.py:771-774, 663-668)

event_representation.how_events_carry_content: generated_world.SemanticWorldEvent (generated_world.py:77-101): semantic_type_id references the BRANCH schema (never a global registry); exact_content preserves the actor's literal words/artifact text; structured_fields are schema-validated typed fields; source_actor_id + direct_targets + intended_visibility (public|participants|private) capture the social envelope; actual_observability maps recipient->representation (generated_world.py:92); linked_action_id + parent_event_ids + cascade_depth + branch_id give causal provenance; schema_id/schema_version pin it to the frozen schema. It is DATA about the world handed to the control plane — it never mutates state itself (the kernel has no per-type handler, generated_world.py:212-213). The fixed baseline instead carries content through primitives like publish_artifact/deliver_information which create WorldObjects + InformationItems (semantic_consequences.py:282-339)
extension_points: GeneratedActionCompiler.compile (generated_world.py:414) — THE action->kernel-ops seam; a scenario ACTION language plugs in here, replacing/augmenting the _ACTION_COMPILE_PROMPT LLM path (generated_world.py:355) and the deterministic scaffolding fallback with a scenario-native compiler that still emits ONLY closed KERNEL ops

KERNEL op registry (generated_world.py:310-316) — the CLOSED set of storage/integrity primitives the language compiles down to; emit_semantic_event/schedule_semantic_event (generated_world.py:211,256) are the event-producing ops
schema.semantic_event_types + schema.record_types() (scenario_schema.py:67,93) — the branch-local vocabulary the language must target; validated at emit time (generated_world.py:137,217)

declare_schema_definition kernel op -> extend_schema (generated_world.py:292, scenario_schema.py:471) — runtime, versioned, ancestry-preserving vocabulary extension when the language needs a type the frozen schema lacks (never rewrites past definitions, scenario_schema.py:486-489)

schema.information_rules (scenario_schema.py:77) consumed by route_semantic_event (generated_world.py:521-529) — the channel/visibility/delay knobs that turn an emitted event into per-recipient deliveries; the language can shape reach through intended_visibility + direct_targets on the emitted event

actor_roles affordances (scenario_schema.py:78) surfaced as EXAMPLE candidate_actions at invocation (generated_world.py:732-736) — the language can widen an actor's feasible action menu without making it a required menu

fields: changes: list[{path, before, after}] — typed values, the machine-readable diff, appended via delta.change() (transitions.py:47,55-57)
uncertainty: dict — carries p_dist, trace_id, consequence_compiler, kernel_quarantined etc (transitions.py:49; phase4_execution.py:214-215)
follow_up_events: list[{etype, ts, participants, payload}] — endogenous events this transition causes (transitions.py:51-53)
state_delta.follow_up_semantics: Follow-ups are the A4 endogenous action->event chain. An operator returns them on the delta; RolloutEngine.run_branch validates each against the event-type registry, forbids travel back in time (ts=max(fu.ts, now), rollout.py:62), horizon-caps via the queue, and schedules valid ones with source='endogenous:<op>' — invalid proposals are logged as rejection deltas, never silently queued (rollout.py:60-73). This ONE path is how every downstream consequence propagates: generated mode rides the emitted ctrl_semantic_event on it (phase4_execution.py:147,153), the router rides ctrl_deliver_observation + ctrl_invoke_actor on it (generated_world.py:572,688), and observation delivery rides the message_delivered->decision_opportunity chain on it (semantic_consequences.py:1055-1062). MatchedRolloutEngine preserves identical follow-up semantics (crn.py:126-139)

observable_view.function: PRODUCTION: phase4_policy.ActorViewBuilder.build -> ActorView (phase4_policy.py:342,382), a fail-closed projection from omniscient state to actor-local state, used by both runtimes (phase4_execution.py:48,76,101; qualitative_actor.py:898). LEGACY/decision-op: transitions.observable_view (transitions.py:123). GENERATED delivery boundary: GeneratedObservationDeliveryOperator only exposes what the router delivered into world.information (generated_world.py:675-683)
observable_view.what_it_hides: ActorView excludes PRIVATE_ACTOR_FIELDS {private_information, latent_state} and SIMULATOR_ONLY_FIELDS (phase4_policy.py:180,390); information is time-gated to world.information.visible_to(actor_id, at=now) and drops items whose published_at > now (phase4_policy.py:418-421); only PUBLIC process quantities (pathway_progress:/mode_progress:/population_aggregate:/nonlinear_state) are projected as beliefs, while outcome/readout machinery (absorption stamps, sampled coefficients, provisional flags) is simulator-only and NEVER enters the view (phase4_policy.py:398-408). Per transitions.observable_view docstring: hides other actors' private fields, latent ground truth (items never exposed to the actor), future queue events, anything post-as-of, and simulator metadata like particle weights and sampled-latent records (transitions.py:123-127)

phase13_matched_rollout.how_crn_wraps_engine: phase13.crn.MatchedRolloutEngine (crn.py:91) SUBCLASSES rollout.RolloutEngine with the SAME event loop, operators, StateDelta and follow_up semantics — only RNG ROUTING differs (crn.py:93-95). It drives a StreamRNG (crn.py:27) that derives an independent deterministic substream per purpose via sha256(root_seed|name): 'hazard|rearm' for queue re-arming (crn.py:102), 'op|<operator>|<etype>' for each operator's draws (crn.py:114,120), 'impl|<action_id>' for implementation-failure draws (interventions.py:108), so an intervention that adds an event cannot desynchronize unrelated exogenous shocks across arms. phase13.counterfactual.MatchedEvaluator (counterfactual.py:46) owns ONE sampled particle set; from_plan builds through the SAME materialize funnel (build_world, check_readout_binding, _inject_posterior_rate, operators_from_plan, queue_builder_from_plan; counterfactual.py:68-85) and auto-appends DecisionActionOperator (counterfactual.py:56-57); evaluate_arm clones each shared particle, applies the intervention to the clone's queue only, and rolls with MatchedRolloutEngine at seed*7919+i identical across arms (counterfactual.py:121-135). Paired report = per-particle U(action_j)-U(reference_j) (counterfactual.py:40-43,192)
phase13_matched_rollout.what_phase13_events_look_like: A candidate action is wrapped by to_intervention (interventions.py:42) whose apply(world, queue) NEVER mutates state — it schedules a single decision_action Event (etype='decision_action', payload={action, problem, source_decision}, source='phase13:intervention', interventions.py:54-58); the do_nothing reference arm schedules nothing (interventions.py:52-53); time-family ops (delay/accelerate/cancel/pause) do queue surgery via _retime_queue (interventions.py:50,66). DecisionActionOperator (interventions.py:82) executes decision_action through the canonical propose->validate->apply funnel: fire-time state-dependent precondition recheck (interventions.py:96-105), implementation-failure draw off impl|<action_id> (interventions.py:107-113), institutional revalidation (interventions.py:121-128), then typed effects — resource consume/transfer, quantity set, and information publish/expose into the ledger (interventions.py:143-188). NOTE: phase13's DecisionActionOperator is a SEPARATE, simpler action executor than the generated kernel; a generated ACTION language would want phase13 arms to run the SAME GeneratedActionCompiler path so counterfactual arms and forward rollout share one action semantics

pr114_patterns_to_borrow.pattern: First-class SemanticEvent that preserves exact causal content and splits intended_audience (source intent) from actual_recipients (router-filled) — SemanticEvent dataclass with exact_content, semantic_content{commitments,intent,ambiguity,timing}, channel, observability, credibility_context, institutional_context, parent_event_ids, plus semantic_signature() for dedup
pr114_patterns_to_borrow.why_good: Preserves exactly what social causality runs on (who said what, to whom, through which channel, with what commitments/ambiguity) instead of an anonymous scalar nudge; the intended-vs-actual audience split is precisely what lets leaks/misses/summaries be represented honestly. The current branch's SemanticWorldEvent (generated_world.py:77-101) already has exact_content + actual_observability but should adopt PR114's explicit intended_audience/actual_recipients split and the semantic_signature() dedup key (the current branch does per-(actor,event_id) dedup in generated_world.py:583, which is coarser than content-gist dedup)

pr114_patterns_to_borrow.pattern: Observation delivery that OWNS information reach and writes every delivery into the InformationLedger with provenance back to the source event — reach is structural (observability class -> institutional boundary -> network edges), time-gated, with representation degradation (original/summary/relayed), perceived_source ('reported:<actor>' for second-hand) and a DeliveredObservation record
pr114_patterns_to_borrow.why_good: The LLM actor never decides whether information reached itself; reach follows structure deterministically so architecture tests replay exactly, and second-hand/summarized reception is modeled as distortion rather than perfect telepathy. This is richer than the current branch's route_semantic_event + GeneratedObservationDeliveryOperator (generated_world.py:506-536,662-683), which degrade only public content to a truncated summary and do not model perceived_source or per-edge relay delay

pr114_patterns_to_borrow.pattern: Reaction propagation exclusively through StateDelta.follow_up_events with structural termination and an auditable cascade manifest — SemanticPropagationEngine.propagate returns follow-up event RECORDS (never touches the queue), enforces semantic-signature dedup, per-branch cascade+depth budgets, one reconsideration per (actor,source-event), and records every quiescence/suppression reason
pr114_patterns_to_borrow.why_good: The reaction closes the loop through the SAME A4 follow_up_events path every endogenous chain uses (identical to the current branch's design, phase4_execution.py:153 + generated_world.py:572), so there is exactly ONE reaction route and termination is structural not hopeful. The current branch tracks budgets in uncertainty_meta['pending_reconsiderations'] (generated_world.py:584) but does not emit the explicit per-branch event_cascade manifest with named quiescence reasons — worth borrowing for provenance

pr114_patterns_to_borrow.pattern: Causal-frontier discovery gated by an INFORMATION GATE and tiering (1/2/3) where every non-cognition substitution is STAMPED into an approximation manifest — CausalFrontierDiscovery.discover promotes only actors whose information actually changed (received the observation, direct target, or institutional standing on an institutional event); everyone causally-adjacent-but-uninformed is stamped, never silently dropped
pr114_patterns_to_borrow.why_good: Prevents actors reacting to events they never observed, and makes every Tier-3/budget approximation visible with reason+sensitivity. The current branch's discover_causal_frontier (generated_world.py:604-645) has the deterministic base (targets, institutional holders, public network neighbors) but NO explicit information gate or approximation manifest — an actor named on the frontier could be invoked without having actually received the observation

pr114_patterns_to_avoid.pattern: Fixed global ontology catalog + action->event-type coefficient mapping — a hardcoded SEMANTIC_EVENT_TYPES catalog of ~18 developer-named types and a fixed _ACTION_EVENT_TYPES dict mapping action names (support->public_endorsement, accept->concession, veto/approve->formal_vote_cast, ...) to that catalog
pr114_patterns_to_avoid.why_bad: This is exactly the global semantic catalog the generated architecture forbids: 'There is NO global catalog of object types, event names, process stages, or reaction menus here' (generated_world.py doc:12-16). Each scenario must GENERATE its own semantic_event_types (scenario_schema.py:67) named for what they ARE in that scenario (compiler prompt scenario_schema.py:282-290). A fixed 18-type ontology reintroduces developer-fixed meaning and would make every scenario's events collapse into the same generic buckets

pr114_patterns_to_avoid.pattern: Fixed numeric coefficient priors baked into module constants — channel->credibility numbers, hardcoded summary thresholds/lengths, fixed relay delay, and a fixed attention cutoff used as delivery/perception coefficients
pr114_patterns_to_avoid.why_bad: These are developer-fixed numeric mappings inside the delivery kernel — the same class of fixed coefficient the generated world keeps OUT of its kernel. In the generated architecture such knobs must come from the scenario's schema.information_rules (read at generated_world.py:521-529: default_channel, default_delay_s, public_channel, public_delay_s, public_representation), not from module globals, so credibility/latency/summarization are scenario-generated and auditable rather than a hidden constant

pr114_patterns_to_avoid.pattern: Lexical polarity vote mapping — collapsing institutional decisions by string-matching action verbs to yes/no (support/approve/accept->yes, oppose/reject/veto/defect->no) and collapsing distinct institutional acts (approve, veto) into one lexically-keyed event type
pr114_patterns_to_avoid.file: PR114 swm/world_model_v2/transitions.py:396-402 (InstitutionalVoteOperator lexical polarity, 'the compiler's own lexical polarity'); PR114 semantic_events.py:56-57 (veto/approve both -> formal_vote_cast). Current-branch lineage has the same sets at generated_world.py:778-781 and transitions.py:389-391
pr114_patterns_to_avoid.why_bad: String-matching free-text actions to a fixed yes/no polarity is brittle and language-fixed: it silently mis-scores any decision phrased outside the hardcoded verb sets and erases institution-specific position semantics. The generated architecture already carries the right substrate — institutional_definitions.decision_record_type with typed position fields and executable aggregation arithmetic (scenario_schema.py:71,204-209; run_institutional_aggregation reads decision records at generated_world.py:761-801). A generated ACTION language should have holders write TYPED decision records (position/status fields defined by the schema) that the aggregation counts, NOT re-derive polarity by lexical matching of the actor's action verb

integration_recommendation.where_a_generated_action_language_should: At the GeneratedActionCompiler.compile seam (generated_world.py:414), which is the SOLE action->direct-effect compiler in generated mode, invoked from ActorPolicyRuntime._apply_consequences (phase4_execution.py:199). Introduce the compiled scenario ACTION language as the deterministic body of this compiler (or a validated pre-pass that produces kernel ops), keeping the LLM path as an untrusted proposer and the UNMODELED_EVENT_TYPE scaffolding as the loud fallback (generated_world.py:483-495). Do NOT add a parallel operator or a second event system — everything must still land on the existing KERNEL (generated_world.py:310) and StateDelta so all downstream routing/observation/invocation is reused unchanged. Also route phase13 counterfactual arms through the same compiler (today phase13 uses the separate DecisionActionOperator, interventions.py:82) so forward rollout and matched rollout share ONE action semantics
integration_recommendation.how_compiled_scenario_actions_should_bec: Compile each ACTION into emit_semantic_event / schedule_semantic_event kernel ops (generated_world.py:211,256) whose semantic_type_id is a type in the BRANCH schema.semantic_event_types (scenario_schema.py:67), carrying the actor's literal words in exact_content and schema-declared typed structured_fields, with intended_visibility + direct_targets set from the action's audience. Preserve exact content/channel/audience/observability the way PR114's SemanticEvent does (borrow its intended_audience-vs-actual_recipients split and semantic_signature dedup, semantic_events.py:87-114) but NEVER against a fixed catalog — if the action needs a type the frozen schema lacks, emit declare_schema_definition FIRST (generated_world.py:292 -> extend_schema scenario_schema.py:471), which versions the schema branch-locally and preserves ancestry
integration_recommendation.how_direct_effects_should_become_state_d: Only the actor's OWN successful performance is a direct effect; express it as create_or_update_record / remove_record / create_or_remove_relation / transfer_conserved_quantity kernel ops (generated_world.py:310-316), each of which lands a delta.change on the shared StateDelta (generated_world.py:154,167,208,281). Every field must be schema-declared and pass _validate_fields (generated_world.py:115-130); numeric/terminal fields are structurally rejected by _FORBIDDEN_KEYS (semantic_consequences.py:67). Institutional decisions must be TYPED decision records the schema defines (decision_record_type position/status fields) counted by run_institutional_aggregation (generated_world.py:761-801) — never a lexical yes/no or a scalar. The question answer stays a read-only outcome_predicate readout over world.objects (generated_world.py:805), never a written effect
integration_recommendation.how_actor_reactions_close_the_loop: The language must express a reaction as OPENING the affected actor's decision, never as asserting their response. Emitting a semantic event is sufficient: it rides delta.follow_up_events (phase4_execution.py:153) -> GeneratedSemanticEventOperator routes it + discovers the frontier (generated_world.py:554) -> GeneratedObservationDeliveryOperator delivers it into the recipient's information ledger (generated_world.py:662) -> the recipient's ctrl_invoke_actor invokes THEIR runtime.decide (generated_world.py:710). Adopt PR114's information gate (causal_frontier.py:115-132) so a frontier actor is only invoked if their information actually changed, and PR114's cascade manifest + quiescence stamping (actor_propagation.py:90-174) for auditable termination. Any op that tries to write another human's belief/support/vote is quarantined by _MIND_WRITE (generated_world.py:318,338-342) and must be avoided by construction
risks: MIND-WRITE guard: any ACTION that tries to encode another actor's reaction (belief/support/compliance/vote/stance/trust) will be quarantined and counted against human_reactions_written_directly (generated_world.py:318-342,72). The language grammar must have no primitive that writes a non-self actor's mind — reactions are ONLY 'open their decision'

NUMERIC/TERMINAL minting: _FORBIDDEN_KEYS blocks probability/utility/belief_delta/pathway_progress/terminal/forecast/outcome_label/ground_truth field names anywhere (semantic_consequences.py:67-69, generated_world.py:121); the language cannot emit scalars, progress bars, or the answer

SCHEMA FIDELITY / vocabulary drift: ops referencing record/event types or fields not in the frozen schema are rejected (generated_world.py:137,217,123-126); the language's vocabulary MUST be the branch schema's or must call declare_schema_definition, and extensions may not redefine existing types (scenario_schema.py:486-489)

BUDGETS truncate cascades LOUDLY: deep action programs / reaction chains hit max_semantic_events (generated_world.py:224), max_invocations_per_actor (generated_world.py:718), and max_cascade_depth (generated_world.py:590); a language that fans out widely will be cut and stamped, changing the terminal distribution

NO-LLM DEGRADATION: without an LLM backend the scenario schema never binds (materialize.py:408-411) and generated mode degrades to the fixed-v1 baseline (phase4_execution.py:217-230, stamped generated_mode_degraded_to_fixed_v1); a generated ACTION language MUST have a fully deterministic compile path so it still runs (and so phase13/architecture tests replay) when no LLM is available

TWO ACTION EXECUTORS today: forward rollout uses GeneratedActionCompiler+kernel while phase13 matched rollout uses DecisionActionOperator (interventions.py:82) — if the language only hooks the former, counterfactual arms will execute actions with DIFFERENT semantics than the forward simulation, breaking the paired-comparison contract (counterfactual.py:5-8). Unify both on the generated compiler

OBSERVATION FIDELITY GAP: the current generated router (generated_world.py:506-536) is thinner than PR114's (no perceived_source, no per-edge relay delay, coarse public-only summarization); if the language relies on rich reach semantics, that router needs PR114's structural-reach upgrades first, sourced from schema.information_rules rather than fixed constants
